evals.prompt: Enable group scanning in my MatrixScan Count app so the user can split the count into one group per pallet with Next Group / Redo controls. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.groupScanningEnabled = true on the BarcodeCountSettings before constructing the BarcodeCount mode, which adds the Next Group / Redo controls. It notes results still come back as a flat list via the normal listener.

assertions.text: settings.groupScanningEnabled = true is set on the BarcodeCountSettings
It is set before BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext(...) is called
It does not invent a separate Next Group / Redo button wiring — the controls come from enabling the setting
It notes (or implies) results still arrive as a flat list via the normal BarcodeCountListener (no grouped-result callback)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count app with group scanning on, relabel the Next Group button to 'Next pallet' and the Redo button to 'Clear pallet'. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill calls barcodeCountView.setNextGroupButtonText("Next pallet") and barcodeCountView.setRedoButtonText("Clear pallet") — the method form (these are setter methods, not Kotlin properties).

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.setNextGroupButtonText("Next pallet") is called (the method form, not a property assignment)
barcodeCountView.setRedoButtonText("Clear pallet") is called (the method form)
It does not assign these as Kotlin properties (e.g. barcodeCountView.nextGroupButtonText = ...) — they are setter methods
